learningPaths.summary: Learn how to migrate resources to Azure, how to provide site recovery for your applications in Azure and on-premises, and how to your applications are available through service interruptions and changes in load.
learningPaths.title: Architect migration, business continuity, and disaster recovery in Azure

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to secure your Azure apps and associated data with encryption, certificates, and policy.
learningPaths.title: Secure your cloud applications in Azure

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to work with Azure Arc enabled servers, data services and Kubernetes.
learningPaths.title: Bring Azure innovation to your hybrid environments with Azure Arc

learningPaths.summary: SC-100: Design security operations, identity, and compliance capabilities
learningPaths.title: Design security operations, identity, and compliance capabilities

learningPaths.summary: Ensure business continuity and implement disaster recovery for SAP solutions on Azure​
learningPaths.title: Ensure business continuity and implement disaster recovery for SAP solutions on Azure​

learningPaths.summary: Learn the fundamentals of datacenters, their roles, components, sustainability, and career opportunities.
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Datacenter

learningPaths.summary: Explore network traffic filtering with Network Security Group, set up Microsoft Defender for Cloud, create a Log Analytics workspace, configure Log Analytics agent integration, Azure Key Vault networking, and connect an Azure SQL server using Azure Private Endpoint in the Azure portal. Enhance cloud security effectively. (SC-5002)
learningPaths.title: Secure Azure services and workloads with Microsoft Defender for Cloud regulatory compliance controls

learningPaths.summary: Explore Azure AI Foundry's tools for generative AI security, privacy, and quality. Shift from reactive risk management to agile, responsible-by-design governance for better observability and efficiency.
learningPaths.title: Operationalize AI responsibly with Azure AI Foundry

learningPaths.summary: Learn about the Microsoft Learn for Educators program and how to best deliver its content to your students. Prepare to deliver Developing solution for Microsoft Azure solutions in the classroom.
learningPaths.title: Prepare to teach PL-300: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst

learningPaths.summary: This learning path explains how to efficiently manage the most commonly used services in Microsoft 365, such as Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft Exchange Online, Microsoft SharePoint Online, and Microsoft Teams.
learningPaths.title: Manage Microsoft 365 services by using Windows PowerShell

learningPaths.summary: Introduction to Microsoft Azure Data non-relational data in Azure
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Microsoft Azure Data non-relational data in Azure

learningPaths.summary: Data must be available when needed. Configure, test and manage a solution for high availability and disaster recovery using Azure tools and SQL Server tools.
learningPaths.title: Plan and implement a high availability and disaster recovery environment

learningPaths.summary: Explore Azure for SAP workloads
learningPaths.title: Explore Azure for SAP workloads

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to design highly reliable, available, and resilient workloads in Azure. Apply core design principles to workloads that typically target maximum uptime.
learningPaths.title: Build mission-critical workloads on Azure

learningPaths.summary: This learning path explains the purpose and use of the PowerShell pipeline and use the pipeline to sort, filter, enumerate and display output data for PowerShell cmdlets.
learningPaths.title: Work with the Windows PowerShell pipeline

learningPaths.summary: Learn how you can migrate your Oracle workloads to Azure.
learningPaths.title: Migrate Oracle workloads to Azure

learningPaths.summary: Implement an Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure
learningPaths.title: Implement an Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure

learningPaths.summary: Administer remote computers by using Windows PowerShell
learningPaths.title: Administer remote computers by using Windows PowerShell

learningPaths.summary: Introduction to Microsoft Azure Data analytics in Azure
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Microsoft Azure Data analytics in Azure

learningPaths.summary: Protect compute, storage, and databases
learningPaths.title: Protect compute, storage, and databases

learningPaths.summary: This learning path helps you leverage the built-in Azure services for securing app data, ensuring that only authorized services and clients have access.
learningPaths.title: Secure your cloud data

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to protect and harden your virtual machines in Azure
learningPaths.title: Implement virtual machine host security in Azure

learningPaths.summary: The Microsoft commercial marketplace is a catalog of cloud applications and services that contains offerings from Microsoft and thousands of partners. In this learning path, you'll be introduced to concepts that will help your organization get ready to sell your cloud products and services through the commercial marketplace.
learningPaths.title: Sell through the commercial marketplace

learningPaths.summary: Learn about the AI Center of Excellence (AI CoE), how it centralizes information about AI adoption, operations, governance, best practices, and security.
learningPaths.title: AI Center of Excellence

learningPaths.summary: In this learning path, you’ll learn how to deploy and configure Azure IaaS Virtual Machine workloads. After completing the learning path, you’ll know how to plan, deploy, and upgrade Windows Server Azure Virtual Machines.
learningPaths.title: Deploy and configure Windows Server IaaS Virtual Machine

learningPaths.summary: Learn to manage and diagnose a network with Azure network foundation services.
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Azure network foundation services

learningPaths.summary: Large-Scale Data Processing with Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2
learningPaths.title: Large-Scale Data Processing with Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to architect a secure, flexible, network infrastructure in Azure and connect on-premises networks to your Azure resources.
learningPaths.title: Architect network infrastructure in Azure

learningPaths.summary: Introductory learning path that is part of the Azure Infrastructure fundamentals content.
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Cloud Infrastructure: Describe cloud concepts

learningPaths.summary: Learn how to design and build secure, scalable, high-performing solutions in Azure using the pillars of the Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Framework.
learningPaths.title: Build great solutions with the Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Framework

learningPaths.summary: In this learning path, you learn about the relationship between Microsoft and SAP across the enterprise software landscape, driving technological innovation and delivering significant benefits to customers. Areas of focus include cloud migration with the RISE with SAP program, AI integration, enhancing workplace productivity, security enhancements, IoT collaboration, and data analytics.
learningPaths.title: Run SAP on the Microsoft Cloud

learningPaths.summary: Learn to manage AI infrastructure by planning compute capacity, enforcing governance, configuring monitoring, and optimizing costs to support scalable, compliant, and high-performing AI workloads without budget overruns or operational risks.
learningPaths.title: Manage AI-ready infrastructure

learningPaths.summary: SC-900: Introduction to Microsoft Priva and Microsoft Purview
learningPaths.title: Introduction to Microsoft Priva and Microsoft Purview

learningPaths.summary: This learning path explains two technologies: PowerShell providers and PowerShell drives, which let you work with many forms of storage by using the commands and techniques that you use to manage the file system.
learningPaths.title: Work with PowerShell providers and PowerShell drives in Windows PowerShell

learningPaths.summary: Learn about the best practices for developing Azure-enabled mixed reality applications with the Mixed Reality Toolkit.
learningPaths.title: HoloLens 2 fundamentals: develop mixed reality applications

learningPaths.summary: This learning path introduces you to Windows PowerShell and provides an overview of the product’s functionality. It explains how to open and configure Windows PowerShell, run commands, and use its built-in Help system.
learningPaths.title: Get started with Windows PowerShell

learningPaths.summary: Fusion development allows your business to build better applications, faster by bringing together professional developers with citizen, or low-code, developers. This learning path will teach you how a fusion development team functions, introduce low-code concepts for professional developers, and explain how a professional developer can enable their citizen developer counterparts to build amazing Power Apps applications using pro-code tools and frameworks they're already familiar and comfortable with, like JavaScript, ASP.NET Core web APIs, and Azure API Management.
learningPaths.title: Transform your business applications with fusion development

learningPaths.summary: This learning path introduces you to connected Field Service and Azure IoT. Included in this learning path is identifying exceptions, register and manage devices, remotely monitor and service equipment, and using IoT central with connected Field Service.
learningPaths.title: Working with Connected Field Service for Dynamics 365 and Azure IoT

learningPaths.summary: Azure Virtual Desktop on Microsoft Azure is a desktop and app virtualization service that runs on the cloud. Azure Virtual Desktop works across devices – including Windows, Mac, iOS, and Android – with full-featured apps that you can use to access remote desktops and apps.
learningPaths.title: Deliver remote desktops and apps with Azure Virtual Desktop

learningPaths.summary: Prepare to build AI agents and extend Microsoft 365 Copilot using Copilot Studio, Microsoft Foundry, Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit, and more. Explore agent use cases, development paths, tools, and related considerations.
learningPaths.title: Build a foundation to build AI agents and extend Microsoft 365 Copilot

learningPaths.summary: Secure software development means integrating security into each phase of your development lifecycle, from requirements analysis to maintenance. Microsoft provides many services that can help you develop more secure code and deploy a more secure application in the cloud. This learning path provides an overview of the services and offerings available to help you build secure software as part of a cybersecurity solution.The deadline for agencies to comply with NIST/CISA/OMB guidance on security measures for critical software (including applying practices of least privilege, network segmentation, and proper configuration), per section 4 of the Executive Order on Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity, is **August 10, 2021**. The deadline for agencies to comply with the guidance identifying practices that enhance the security of the software supply chain with respect to software procurement, also per section 4, is **March 8, 2022**.
learningPaths.title: Learn how Microsoft supports secure software development as part of a cybersecurity solution

learningPaths.summary: Know your data, protect your data, and don't lose your data. These are the basic tenets of good information protection - data discovery, classification, and protection. This learning path provides an overview of the Microsoft data protection capabilities and how to use them to protect and govern data as part of a cybersecurity solution.The deadline for the heads of federal civilian agencies to evaluate and classify agency data and provide to DHS and OMB a report of their evaluation, per Section 8 of the Executive Order on Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity, is **August 24, 2021**.
learningPaths.title: Learn how Microsoft supports data discovery, classification, and protection as part of a cybersecurity solution

learningPaths.summary: Multifactor authentication helps secure your environment and resources by requiring that your users confirm their identity by using multiple authentication methods, like a phone call, text message, mobile app notification, or one-time password. You can use multifactor authentication both on-premises and in the cloud to add security for accessing Microsoft online services, remote access applications, and more. This learning path provides an overview of how to use multifactor authentication as part of a cybersecurity solution.The deadline for agencies to adopt multifactor authentication and encryption at rest and in transit to the maximum extent (and report on progress every 60 days), per Section 3 of the Executive Order on Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity, is **November 8, 2021**.
learningPaths.title: Learn how Microsoft supports using multifactor authentication as part of a cybersecurity solution
